title: ingester: channels joined after boot don't receive live NewMessage events
description:
ROOT CAUSE: backend/src/ingester/main.py:63-67 calls subscribe_to_active_channels() ONCE at startup. subscribe_to_active_channels (live.py:105-130) loads chat_id list once into chat_map and registers handler with events.NewMessage(chats=chat_ids) — that list is frozen for the process lifetime. join_worker._handle_one_pending (join_worker.py:64-155) creates new Channel/ChannelSubscription rows and runs _backfill_channel (50 messages) but never re-registers the live handler or extends chat_map. Result: a channel joined after ingester boot gets its initial backfill but NO live updates until ingester restart (or next manual catchup_channels at boot).

REPRODUCE:
1. Start ingester. List of active channels frozen.
2. Through TMA, add a new channel via /sources.
3. join_worker joins it, backfills 50 messages — those appear in /feed.
4. New post is published in that channel.
5. Wait, refresh — new post never appears in BD/feed (live handler ignored it).
6. Restart ingester — catchup_channels fires, new post appears.

FIX OPTIONS:
A) Pass a shared mutable chat_map dict into both live.py handler and join_worker. Remove chats=... filter from events.NewMessage so handler sees ALL events; filter inside on_new_message via current dict. join_worker mutates the dict after successful join.
B) After successful join in join_worker, call client.remove_event_handler + subscribe_to_active_channels again to re-register with updated list.
C) Periodic re-subscribe loop (every N minutes).

RECOMMEND: option A — cleanest, no handler churn, no extra DB hits.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Add unit test that join_worker successfully causes a subsequent NewMessage for the newly-joined channel to be handled (mock TelegramClient with fake NewMessage event).
- Existing live.py tests still pass.

title: feed Redis cache 30s blocks fresh posts on Refresh
description:
ROOT CAUSE: backend/src/api/routers/feed.py:16-31 caches INITIAL page in Redis for 30s under key 'feed:{user_id}:INITIAL:{limit}' with no invalidation anywhere in the codebase. When a new post arrives via ingester (live.py upsert_post), nothing busts the cache. User hits Refresh in TMA, useInfiniteQuery refetch() fires fresh HTTP request, backend returns cached stale page → new post invisible for up to 30s.

REPRODUCE:
1. Open TMA feed (cache fills with INITIAL page).
2. New post arrives in subscribed channel within 30s.
3. Tap Refresh.
4. New post does NOT appear (returns cached page).
5. Wait 30s+, tap Refresh again → new post appears.

FIX OPTIONS:
A) Skip cache entirely when cursor is None (INITIAL). Paginated pages (cursor != None) remain cacheable because keyset data below the cursor is immutable.
B) Invalidate user's INITIAL key from ingester when upsert_post returns new_id (requires plumbing Redis into ingester).
C) Reduce TTL to ~3s (palliative, still has window).

RECOMMEND: option A for MVP. Cleanest, no cross-service plumbing.

ACCEPTANCE:
- New integration test: insert Post after first /feed call within TTL window → second /feed call must include it.
- Existing /feed tests still pass.
- Refresh in TMA shows new posts immediately.
close_reason: Fixed: /feed router bypasses Redis cache when cursor is None (INITIAL page). Paginated pages (cursor != None) remain cacheable because keyset data below the cursor is immutable. New integration test test_refresh_returns_fresh_post_within_cache_window verifies the bug repro and prevents regression. Full integration suite (55) + unit/smoke (70) green.

title: [bug] У друга канал зависает в queued, исчезает из UI, не появляется в /sources
description: Симптом: на основных аккаунтах автора добавление @username работает; у друга на zupperik.dev TMA показывает 'Queued (pending)…', потом исчезает, канал не появляется в GET /sources. Поток: POST /sources → 202 queued → polling GET /sources/queue/{id}. queueId хранится в useState локально в useAddSource — навигация сбрасывает UI до idle. Подозрение: pending row застряла в channel_join_queue (status=pending forever), join_worker get_entity (join_worker.py:81) ловит только 4 ошибки, остальные exception'ы пробрасываются → rollback → row снова pending → infinite loop. Нужно: SELECT из channel_join_queue по friend.tg_user_id, docker logs ingester --tail=500 за время попытки, проверить есть ли user row для friend.tg_user_id, проверить статус последних queue rows для друга.
acceptance_criteria: root cause найден через логи или БД; описан в beads-задаче перед предложением фикса
close_reason: Root cause: orphan 'docker compose run ingester' container (label oneoff=True, name telegram-feed-ingester-run-85ecb2582b33) held SQLite lock on Telethon session file for 2h. Main telegram-feed-ingester-1 crashed 421x with sqlite3.OperationalError: database is locked, never reached join_worker.started — POST /sources sat in pending forever for everyone (visible only when *another* user's pending was racing/colliding). Fix: docker rm -f the oneoff container. Verified: queue id=10 (ntenews / user_id=5 arcane_ars) processed in 2s, user_sources row created, join_worker.joined event in logs. Friend confirms 'все заработало'. Followups filed: telegram-feed-aqc (prevent recurrence), telegram-feed-7h6 (live.catchup_get_entity_failed PeerUser), telegram-feed-m4k (document .deploy/ SSH workflow).

title: Dockerfile missing COPY backend/scripts — create_admin.sh fails
description: bash infra/scripts/create_admin.sh blows up with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'scripts'. Root cause: backend/Dockerfile copies only backend/src into the image. backend/scripts (which has the create_admin module) never lands in /app. Fix: add COPY backend/scripts ./scripts. WORKDIR=/app makes /app the sys.path[0] for 'python -m', so scripts.create_admin resolves once /app/scripts/__init__.py exists.
close_reason: Added COPY backend/scripts ./scripts to runtime stage of backend/Dockerfile. python -m scripts.create_admin now resolves at /app/scripts via CWD-based sys.path[0].

title: Admin SPA: npm ci fails on fresh checkout due to bundled deps missing from lock
description: On fresh checkout 'npm ci' in frontend/admin fails with 'Missing: @emnapi/core@1.10.0 from lock file' and similar for @emnapi/runtime. These are bundleDependencies of @tailwindcss/oxide-wasm32-wasi@4.3.0 (optional, wasm32-cpu). The committed lock (generated by an older npm) elided bundled-dep entries; newer npm ci is strict and refuses to install. Fix: regenerated package-lock.json with npm 11.6.2 which adds nested inBundle entries (~45 LOC) that pass strict validation. Verified locally with 'npm ci --dry-run' (297 packages added, no errors).
close_reason: Regenerated frontend/admin/package-lock.json with npm 11.6.2 (commit 97195de). Adds nested inBundle entries for @tailwindcss/oxide-wasm32-wasi bundleDeps. npm ci --dry-run passes locally.

title: TMA build fails on fresh checkout: tsc runs before routeTree.gen.ts is generated
description: On a fresh checkout (e.g., VDS during prod deploy via RUNBOOK-prod.md step 5), 'npm run build' in frontend/tma fails with TS2307 'Cannot find module ./routeTree.gen' and TS2345 errors on createFileRoute calls. Root cause: build script is 'tsc --noEmit && vite build'; tsc runs first, but routeTree.gen.ts is gitignored (frontend/tma/.gitignore:7) and only generated by the TanStackRouterVite plugin during vite build. The createFileRoute<'/'> typing depends on the FileRoutesByPath augmentation in the same gen file, so route files fail to typecheck too. Fix: reorder to 'vite build && tsc --noEmit' so vite generates the file first, then tsc validates everything.
close_reason: Reordered build script to 'vite build && tsc --noEmit' in frontend/tma/package.json. Vite plugin now generates routeTree.gen.ts before tsc validates it.

title: Telegram Feed MVP — TMA + ingester + admin
description:
Multi-user Telegram Mini App for aggregated channel feed with backend service for ingest/normalization/storage.

Approved design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-17-telegram-feed-design.md (local-only, not in repo)

Stack:
- Backend: Python 3.12 + FastAPI + Telethon + SQLAlchemy + Alembic
- Frontend (TMA): React 18 + Vite + @telegram-apps/sdk-react + TanStack Query
- Sysadmin: separate React SPA with login + TOTP
- Infra: PostgreSQL 16, MinIO, Redis 7, nginx — all via Docker Compose
- Hosting: VPS for prod, identical local dev stack via docker-compose.override.yml
- Source of posts: MTProto userbot via Telethon (NewMessage events + backfill)

Architecture: split ingester worker + FastAPI; they communicate via PostgreSQL only.

MVP feature scope (per business reqs):
- Telegram auth via initData
- Scrollable feed of posts from connected sources (pull-to-refresh delivery)
- Save / hide post / hide source
- User-managed source list
- Sysadmin panel for system-wide channel management
- Backend ingest, normalization, storage of posts
- Photo media in MinIO, videos via tg:// open-in-Telegram

Out of MVP: search, push, read/unread, video streaming proxy, sharding, archival.

title: [infra] Prevent forgotten docker-compose oneoff ingester from locking Telethon session
description: Prod incident 2026-05-18: a 'docker compose run ingester ...' container (label com.docker.compose.oneoff=True, name telegram-feed-ingester-run-85ecb2582b33) ran orphaned for 2 hours and held an SQLite lock on the Telethon *.session file. Main ingester (telegram-feed-ingester-1) crashed 421 times with sqlite3.OperationalError: database is locked and never reached join_worker.started — POST /sources for all users hung in pending. Need at least one of these mitigations: (1) bootstrap.sh / deploy script sweep before 'docker compose up': docker ps -q --filter 'label=com.docker.compose.oneoff=true' --filter 'name=telegram-feed' | xargs -r docker rm -f. (2) RUNBOOK-prod.md add a step after one-time Telethon phone auth: 'docker compose rm -f ingester' or run auth via dedicated script that does not leave a long-running container. (3) Document the failure mode (Telethon SQLite session is single-writer; do not run two ingester processes against the same session-file). (4) Optional: docker-compose healthcheck on the ingester service that fails if 'database is locked' appears in the last 30s of logs.

title: [bug] Postgres healthcheck спамит FATAL: database tgf does not exist
description: docker-compose.yml: healthcheck postgres использует 'pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER}' без -d. Postgres default подразумевает database name = user name. У нас POSTGRES_USER=tgf, POSTGRES_DB=telegram_feed, БД 'tgf' не существует. Healthcheck всё равно проходит (pg_isready возвращает 0 если сервер отвечает), но в логах каждые 5с спамится 'FATAL: database tgf does not exist'. Fix: добавить -d ${POSTGRES_DB}.
close_reason: Healthcheck postgres получил -d $POSTGRES_DB. Теперь pg_isready подключается к существующей БД telegram_feed, FATAL спам остановится после dcp up -d postgres.

title: Backfill: guarantee at least N posts on first channel subscription
description: When user first subscribes to a channel, ingester runs backfill. Currently the join_worker marks the queue done before backfill completes, and a partial backfill (FloodWait or transient error mid-stream) leaves the queue 'done' with <N posts inserted. Fix: reorder so mark_join_done runs after backfill; return (inserted_count, exhausted) from _backfill_channel; if inserted < MIN_BACKFILL_POSTS AND channel was NOT exhausted, mark queue failed with reason backfill_short:N; retry FloodWaitError up to 3 times inside backfill. Add config knobs: ingester_backfill_limit=50, ingester_backfill_min_posts=10. Tests: exhaustion path, FloodWait retry, short-mark-failed, success.
close_reason: Implemented on branch backfill-floor (commit 3319056). 8/8 unit tests pass. Awaiting user merge approval.

title: Normalize channel.username to lowercase in ingester join_worker
description: API /sources router lowercases the incoming username before SELECT (sources.py:46 'username = body.username.lower()'). The ingester writes whatever case Telethon returns (join_worker.py ~line 129 'username=getattr(entity, ..., None) or username') with no normalization, and upsert_channel does not normalize either. Effect: a user adds '@DurovChannel'; ingester stores 'DurovChannel'; a later POST /sources {username: 'durovchannel'} misses the existing row, re-enqueues an unnecessary join (lands on tg_chat_id conflict so no real harm, but the user gets 'queued' instead of immediate 'subscribed'). Fix: lowercase in join_worker.py before passing username to upsert_channel; Telegram usernames are ASCII [A-Za-z0-9_] so .lower() is unambiguous. Surfaced during Plan 3 Phase 2 code review.

title: Plan 3 follow-up — Redis sliding-window rate limit for /auth and POST /sources
description: Design §6.5 requires /auth/telegram ≤ 5/min/IP and POST /sources ≤ 30/h/user. title: [docs] Document SSH access for ad-hoc prod debugging via .deploy/
description: During 2026-05-18 outage investigation, set up .deploy/ for ad-hoc SSH-based prod debugging from the developer's workstation: .deploy/ssh_config (Host alias 'zupperik' pointing at the VDS), .deploy/id_deploy (private key for deploy user), .deploy/logs/ (captured docker logs / SQL dumps). The entire .deploy/ directory is gitignored at the top-level .gitignore plus a defense-in-depth .deploy/.gitignore. RUNBOOK-prod.md should mention the convention for the next person who needs to debug prod: where keys live, how to add a new debugger key (cat .pub | ssh deploy@... 'cat >> ~/.ssh/authorized_keys'), what NOT to commit. Also: how to revoke a debugger by editing authorized_keys server-side.

title: [bug] live.catchup_get_entity_failed for PeerUser entities (channels 3,4,7,8 on prod)
description: After main ingester restart 2026-05-18T19:33:33Z, live.catchup_done succeeded for channels 1,2 but failed for 3,4,7,8 with: 'Could not find the input entity for PeerUser(user_id=...)'. Errors logged at live.catchup_get_entity_failed level=warning, channels keep working otherwise. Telethon needs to have seen the peer in current process to resolve it; after a cold restart the in-memory entity cache is empty for users who only appear as message authors. Fix candidates: (1) Pre-warm entities via client.get_dialogs() on startup so the entity cache includes all channels + their recent senders. (2) In live handler, on get_entity failure, fetch via client.get_input_entity(PeerChannel(channel_id)) instead of resolving the author PeerUser. (3) Accept the warning as benign if downstream behavior is fine — needs verification that posts are still ingested correctly despite this warning.

title: TMA typecheck script broken on fresh checkout (same root cause as 7dv)
description: The 'typecheck' script in frontend/tma/package.json is 'tsc --noEmit' standalone, which fails on fresh checkout because routeTree.gen.ts doesn't exist yet (only generated by vite). Build is fixed by reordering (telegram-feed-7dv) but typecheck-only invocations (CI lint gate, IDE-driven type checks before any vite run) still fail. Proper fix: install @tanstack/router-cli as devDep and add 'pretypecheck': 'tsr generate' (and ideally 'prebuild': 'tsr generate' so we can restore fail-fast tsc ordering in build too). Defer until CI workflow is added or someone needs standalone typecheck on fresh clones.

title: apiFetch concurrent-401 refresh deduplication
description: Two parallel apiFetch calls that both hit 401 each fire POST /auth/refresh independently; the second response overwrites the first via setTokens, so the first call's retry may use slightly stale tokens. Acceptable for MVP single-user TMA. Fix sketch: cache an in-flight Promise<TokenPair | null> at module scope and reuse it. Surfaced during Plan 4 Phase 1 code review.

title: Add negative-case tests for /media/{id}
description: test_media_router.py covers only the happy path (302 to presigned). Add tests for: 404 when media_id unknown; 404 when storage_key is None (e.g. a video that never had a thumb); 401 when no Authorization header. Surfaced during Plan 3 Phase 5 code review.

title: Cache MinIO Storage client on app.state.storage_client
description: routers/media.py constructs a fresh Minio client (and urllib3 PoolManager) per request via make_storage_client(). For MVP single-user this is invisible, but under load it's measurable overhead. Build the client once in lifespan, attach to app.state.storage_client (same pattern as engine and redis), and have media.py read from app.state. Surfaced during Plan 3 Phase 5 code review.

title: Make /feed Redis cache writes failure-tolerant
description: routers/feed.py:75 currently lets redis.set exceptions propagate to the client; if Redis is briefly unavailable the route 500s even though the feed data is already computed. Wrap redis.set in try/except and log via structlog so the route degrades gracefully (returns data, skips caching). Surfaced during Plan 3 Phase 3 code review.

title: Lift _make_init_data helper into shared test fixture
description: Phase 0 test_auth_initdata.py and Phase 1 test_auth_router.py each define their own _make_init_data helper + BOT_TOKEN constant. They will drift. Pull both into backend/tests/_helpers/initdata.py (or a conftest fixture) so future auth-related tests share one canonical signer. Surfaced during Phase 1 code review.

title: shared/logging.py: PrintLoggerFactory caches stale stdout under pytest capsys
description: shared/logging.py uses PrintLoggerFactory(file=sys.stdout) with cache_logger_on_first_use=True. The first structlog.get_logger() after configure_logging() caches a PrintLogger bound to current stdout. When pytest's capsys tears down that stdout between tests, subsequent log.warning() calls in production code raise 'I/O operation on closed file'. Workaround in ingester/photos.py wraps log calls in try/except ValueError. Real fix: drop cache_logger_on_first_use OR switch to WriteLoggerFactory (resolves stdout at call time). Discovered during Plan 2 P3.

title: API health endpoint reports version 0.0.0 due to Dockerfile --no-root
description: backend/src/api/main.py reads version via importlib.metadata.version('telegram-feed-backend'). Dockerfile installs deps with --no-root, so the project package metadata is absent at runtime and the fallback '0.0.0' fires. Either drop --no-root from Dockerfile or read version from pyproject.toml / an embedded constant. Endpoint still returns 200; this is cosmetic.

value: Never include Co-Authored-By Claude, 🤖 Generated with Claude Code, Generated-By, Assisted-By in commits or PRs. Settings already configured (attribution.commit=empty, includeCoAuthoredBy=false) but the Bash tool's default HEREDOC template still tries to add it — write commit messages without that trailer.

bd close не auto-export .beads/issues.jsonl при работе из git worktree (только из main worktree). Перед commit изменений после bd close/update нужно явно: bd export --output .beads/issues.jsonl. Это безопасно вызывать даже когда auto-export сработал — overwrite того же контента.

Прод /opt/telegram-feed может быть склонирован под root (несмотря на RUNBOOK §3 chown deploy:deploy /opt/telegram-feed — это только директория, без -R). Симптомы при auto-deploy через ssh deploy@: 'fatal: detected dubious ownership in repository' (git pull блокирован) + 'open /opt/telegram-feed/.env: permission denied' (docker compose не читает .env). Сервисы при этом продолжают работать (Docker socket не требует FS-прав на .env для running containers) — иллюзия, что прод жив, но новый код не выкатывается. Фикс на сервере: sudo chown -R deploy:deploy /opt/telegram-feed. RUNBOOK исправлен коммитом 4c03d8d (sanity-check после git clone). Инцидент 2026-05-18 deploy log: .deploy/logs/deploy-20260518-200936.log → fix → .deploy/logs/deploy-20260518-201431.log.

Telethon хранит auth в SQLite *.session — это SINGLE-WRITER. Два процесса ingester против одного session-файла = locked DB у одного + AUTH_KEY_DUPLICATED rejection от Telegram у другого = оба не работают. Симптомы: ingester restart_count > N, 'sqlite3.OperationalError: database is locked' в стек-трейсе, второй контейнер с label com.docker.compose.oneoff=True (создан 'docker compose run ingester', обычно для интерактивной phone-auth и забыт). Профилактика: после first-time phone-auth — docker compose rm -f ingester. Диагностика: docker ps -a --filter 'label=com.docker.compose.oneoff=true'. Прод-инцидент 2026-05-18 telegram-feed-9s9.
One commit = one closed beads task. Don't commit per TDD cycle or per file. Auto-commit allowed at bd close. Reasoning: per-cycle commits clutter history and hurt code review of a coherent change.

После merge в main и git push origin main агент сам выкатывает на zupperik.dev через ssh -F .deploy/ssh_config zupperik. Краткая инструкция в проектном CLAUDE.md (секция Production Auto-Deploy), полный гайд в docs/DEPLOY.md. Pre-conditions: branch=main, clean, тесты зелёные, push сделан, bd in_progress пусто. Selective rebuild по diff: backend -> build/up api ingester + alembic; frontend/tma -> npm ci && build + restart nginx; frontend/admin -> npm ci && VITE_API_BASE_URL && build + restart nginx; infra/nginx -> restart nginx; compose/Dockerfile -> build && up -d. Verify: curl /internal/health + /api/internal/health + logs --tail=30 каждого сервиса (no ERROR/Traceback). Failure: env-specific можно фиксить on-server, но обязан вернуть фикс в git (drift с origin/main запрещён). Code-specific -> локально. NEVER: feature-branch deploy, force push, auto-rollback, dcp down -v, alembic upgrade без backend/migrations/** в diff. Логи -> .deploy/logs/deploy-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.log. NB: CLAUDE.md и docs/ оба в .gitignore (local-only) — инструкция не шарится через git.

Use EnterWorktree (deferred Claude Code native tool, requires ToolSearch select:EnterWorktree first) for worktrees. NEVER run 'git worktree add' directly — creates phantom worktree invisible to the harness in .worktrees/ instead of .claude/worktrees/.

Windows + Docker Desktop quirks: testcontainers Ryuk reaper flaky on 2nd boot per session (TESTCONTAINERS_RYUK_DISABLED=true workaround in backend/tests/conftest.py). bootstrap.sh uses python3 which resolves to Windows Store stub (telegram-feed-2ls). Always test cross-platform shell commands.
